author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LVII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have heard in the Gospel just now, brethren, how loveth us our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ, God with the Father, Man with us, out of our own selves, now at the right hand of the Father; ye have heard how much He loveth us.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because then this Psalm is singing of the Passion of the Lord, see what is the title that it hath: “at the end.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The end is Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why hath He been called end?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not as one that consumeth, but one that consummateth.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “At the end, corrupt not, for David himself, for the inscription of the title; when he fled from the face of Saul into a cavern.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We referring to holy Scripture, do find indeed how holy David, that king of Israel, from whom too the Psalter of David hath received the name thereof, had suffered for persecutor Saul the king of his own people, as many of you know that have either read or have heard the Scriptures.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: King David had then for persecutor Saul: and whereas the one was most gentle, the other most ferocious: the one mild, the other envious; the one patient, the other cruel; the one beneficent, the other ungrateful: he endured him with so much mildness, that when he had gotten him into his hands him he touched not, hurt not.…What reference hath this to Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If all things which then were being done, were figures of things future, we find there Christ, and by far in the greatest degree.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this, “corrupt not for the inscription of the title,” I see not how it belongeth to that David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not any “title” was inscribed over David himself which Saul would “corrupt.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But we see in the Passion of the Lord that there had been written a title, “King of the Jews:” in order that this title might put to the blush these very men, seeing that from their King they withheld not their hands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in them Saul was, in Christ David was.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Christ, as saith the Apostolic Gospel, is, as we know, as we confess, of the seed of David after the flesh; for after the Godhead He is above David, above all men, above heaven and earth, above angels, above all things visible and invisible.…And because already it had been sung through the Holy Spirit, “Unto the end, corrupt not, for the inscription of the title:” Pilate answered them, “What I have written, I have written:” why do ye suggest to me falsehood?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I corrupt not truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What therefore is, “When he fled from the face of Saul into a cavern”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which thing indeed the former David also did: but because in him we find not the inscription of the title, in the latter let us find the flight into the cavern.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that cavern wherein David hid himself did figure somewhat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But wherefore hid he himself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was in order that he might be concealed and not be found.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is to be hidden in a cavern?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To be hidden in earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he that fleeth into a cavern, with earth is covered so that he may not be seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But Jesus did carry earth, flesh which He had received from earth: and in it He concealed Himself, in order that by Jews He might not be discovered as God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For if they had known, never the Lord of glory would they have crucified.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why therefore the Lord of glory found they not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because in a cavern He had hidden Himself, that is, the flesh’s weakness to their eyes He presented, but the Majesty of the Godhead in the body’s clothing, as though in a hiding-place of the earth, He hid.…But wherefore even unto death willed He to be patient?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was in order that He might flee from the face of Saul into a cavern.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a cavern may be understood as a lower part of the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And certainly, as is manifest and certain to all, His Body in a Tomb was laid, which was cut in a Rock.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Tomb therefore was the Cavern; thither He fled from the face of Saul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so long the Jews did persecute Him, even until He was laid in a cavern.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence prove we that so long they persecuted Him, until therein He was laid?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even when dead, and, on the Cross hanging, with lance they wounded Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when shrouded, the funeral celebrated, He was laid in a cavern, no longer had they anything which to the Flesh they might do.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rose therefore the Lord again out of that cavern unhurt, uncorrupt, from that place whither He had fled from the face of Saul: concealing Himself from ungodly men, whom Saul prefigured, but showing Himself to His members.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the members of Him rising again by His members were handled: for the members of Him, the Apostles, touched Him rising again and believed; and behold nothing profited the persecution of Saul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear we therefore now the Psalm; because concerning the title thereof enough we have spoken, as far as the Lord hath deigned to give.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Have pity on me, O God, have pity on me, for in Thee hath trusted my Soul” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ in the Passion saith, “Have pity on Me, O God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To God, God saith, “Have pity on Me!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that with the Father hath pity on thee, in thee crieth, “Have pity on Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that part of Him which is crying, “Have pity on Me,” is thine: from thee this He received, for the sake of thee, that thou shouldest be delivered, with Flesh He was clothed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The flesh itself crieth: “Have pity on Me, O God, have pity on me:” Man himself, soul and flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whole Man did the Word take upon Him, and whole Man the Word became.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let it not therefore be thought that there Soul was not, because the Evangelist thus saith: “The Word was made flesh, and dwelled in us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For man is called flesh, as in another place saith the Scripture, “And all flesh shall see the salvation of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall anywise flesh alone see, and shall Soul not be there?…Thou hearest the Master praying, learn thou to pray.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to this end He prayed, in order that He might teach how to pray: because to this end He suffered, in order that He might teach how to suffer; to this end He rose again, in order that He might teach how to hope for rising again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And in the shadow of Thy wings I will hope, until iniquity pass over.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This now evidently whole Christ doth say: here is also our voice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not yet hath passed over, still rife is iniquity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And in the end our Lord Himself said there should be an abounding of iniquity: “And since iniquity shall abound, the love of many shall wax cold; but he that shall have persevered unto the end, the same shall be saved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But who shall persevere even unto the end, even until iniquity pass over?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that shall have been in the Body of Christ, he that shall have been in the members of Christ, and from the Head shall have learned the patience of persevering.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou passest away, and behold passed are thy temptations; and thou goest into another life whither have gone holy men, if holy thou hast been.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Into another life have gone Martyrs; if Martyr thou shalt have been, thou also goest into another life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “thou” hast passed away hence, hath by any means iniquity therefore passed away?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are born other unrighteous men, as there die some unrighteous men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In like manner therefore as some unrighteous men die and others are born: so some just men go, and others are born.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even unto the end of the world neither iniquity will be wanting to oppress, nor righteousness to suffer.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will cry to God most high” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If most high He is, how heareth He thee crying?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Confidence hath been engendered by experience: “to God,” he saith, “who had done good to me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If before that I was seeking Him, He did good to me, when I cry shall He not hearken to me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For good to us the Lord God hath done in sending to us our Saviour Jesus Christ, that He might die for our offences, and rise again for our justification.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what sort of men hath He willed His Son to die?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For ungodly men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But ungodly men were not seeking God, and have been sought of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He is Most High in such sort, as that not far from Him is our misery and our groaning: because “near is the Lord to them that have bruised the heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God that hath done good to me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath sent from heaven and hath saved me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now the Man Himself, now the Flesh Itself, now the Son of God after His partaking of ourselves, of Him it is manifest, how He was saved, and hath sent from heaven the Father and hath saved Him, hath sent from heaven, and hath raised Him again: but in order that ye may know, that also the Lord Himself hath raised again Himself; both truths are written in Scripture, both that the Father hath raised Him again, and that Himself Himself hath raised again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear ye how the Father hath raised Him again: the Apostle saith, “He hath been made,” he saith, “obedient unto death, even the death of the Cross: wherefore God also hath exalted Him, and hath given Him a name which is above every name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye have heard of the Father raising again and exalting the Son; hear ye how that He too Himself His flesh hath raised again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Under the figure of a temple He saith to the Jews, “Destroy this Temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the Evangelist hath explained to us what it was that He said: “But this,” he saith, “He spake of the Temple of His Body.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now therefore out of the person of one praying, out of the person of a man, out of the person of the flesh, He saith, “He hath saved me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath given unto reproach those that trampled on me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Them that have trampled on Him, that over Him dead have insulted, that Him as though man have crucified, because God they perceived not, them He hath given unto reproach.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See ye whether it has not been so done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The thing we do not believe as yet to come, but fulfilled we acknowledge it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Jews raged against Christ, they were overbearing against Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the city of Jerusalem.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For where they reigned, there they were puffed up, there their necks they lifted up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After the Passion of the Lord thence they were rooted out; and they lost the kingdom, wherein Christ for King they would not acknowledge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what manner they have been given unto reproach, see ye: dispersed they have been throughout all nations, nowhere having a settlement, nowhere a sure abode.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But for this reason still Jews they are, in order that our books they may carry to their confusion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whenever we wish to show Christ prophesied of, we produce to the heathen these writings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And lest perchance men hard of belief should say that we Christians have composed these books, so that together with the Gospel which we have preached we have forged the Prophet, through whom there might seem to be foretold that which we preach: by this we convince them; namely, that all the very writings wherein Christ hath been prophesied are with the Jews, all these very writings the Jews have.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We produce documents from enemies, to confound other enemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what sort of reproach therefore are the Jews?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A document the Jew carrieth, wherefrom a Christian may believe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our librarians they have become, just as slaves are wont behind their masters to carry documents, in such sort that these faint in carrying, those profit by reading.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unto such a reproach have been given the Jews: and there hath been fulfilled that which so long before hath been foretold, “He hath given unto reproach those that trampled on me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how great a reproach it is, brethren, that this verse they should read, and themselves being blind should look upon their mirror!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in the same manner the Jews appear in the holy Scripture which they carry, as appeareth the face of a blind man in a mirror: by other men it is seen, by himself not seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wast inquiring perhaps when he said, “He hath sent from heaven and hath saved me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What hath He sent from heaven?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whom hath He sent from heaven?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: An Angel hath He sent, to save Christ, and through a servant is the Lord saved?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all Angels are creatures serving Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For obedience there might have been sent Angels, for service they might have been sent, not for succour: as is written, “Angels ministered unto Him,” not like men merciful to one indigent, but like subjects to One Omnipotent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What therefore “hath He sent from heaven, and hath saved me”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now we hear in another verse what from heaven He hath sent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath sent from heaven His mercy and His truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what purpose?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And hath drawn out my soul from the midst of the lions’ whelps.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hath sent,” he saith, “from heaven His mercy and His truth:” and Christ Himself saith, “I am Truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There was sent therefore Truth, that it should draw out my soul hence from the midst of the lions’ whelps: there was sent mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ Himself we find to be both mercy and truth; mercy in suffering with us, and truth in requiting us.…Who are the lions’ whelps?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That lesser people, unto evil deceived, unto evil led away by the chiefs of the Jews: so that these are lions, those lions’ whelps.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All roared, all slew.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we are to hear even here the slaying of these very men, presently in the following verses of this Psalm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And hath drawn out,” he saith, “my soul from the midst of the lions’ whelps” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why sayest thou, “And hath drawn out my soul”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what hadst thou suffered, that thy soul should be drawn out?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have slept troubled.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ hath intimated His death.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence “troubled”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who troubling?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us see in what manner he brandeth an evil conscience upon the Jews, wishing to excuse themselves of the slaying of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to this end, as the Gospel speaketh, to the judge they delivered Him, that they might not themselves seem to have killed Him.…Let us question Him, and say, since Thou hast slept troubled, who have persecuted Thee? who have slain Thee? was it perchance Pilate, who to soldiers gave Thee, on the Tree to be hanged, with nails to be pierced?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear who they were, “Sons of men” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of them He speaketh, whom for persecutors He suffered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how did they slay, that steel bare not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They that sword drew not, that made no assault upon Him to slay; whence slew they?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Their teeth are arms and arrows, and their tongue a sharp sword.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not consider the unarmed hands, but the mouth armed: from thence the sword proceeded, wherewith Christ was to be slain: in like manner also as from the mouth of Christ, that wherewith the Jews were to be slain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He hath a sword twice whetted: and rising again He hath smitten them, and hath severed from them those whom He would make His faithful people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They an evil sword, He a good sword: they evil arrows, He good arrows.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He hath Himself also arrows good, words good, whence He pierceth the faithful heart, in order that He may be loved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore of one kind are their arrows, and of another kind their sword.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sons of men, their teeth are arms and arrows, and their tongue a sharp sabre.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Tongue of sons of men is a sharp sabre, and their teeth arms and arrows.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When therefore did they smite, save when they clamoured, “Crucify, crucify”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what have they done to Thee, O Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the Prophet here exult!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For above, all those verses the Lord was speaking: a Prophet indeed, but in the person of the Lord, because in the Prophet is the Lord.…“Be exalted,” he saith, “above the Heavens, O God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Man on the Cross, and above the Heavens, God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them continue on the earth raging, Thou in Heaven be judging.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where are they that were raging? where are their teeth, the arms and arrows?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Have not “the stripes of them been made the arrows of infants”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in another place a Psalm this saith, desiring to prove them vainly to have raged, and vainly unto frenzies to have been driven headlong: for nothing they were able to do to Christ when for the time crucified, and afterwards when He was rising again, and in Heaven was sitting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How do infants make to themselves arrows?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what arrows? or what powers? or what bows? or what wound?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be Thou exalted above the Heavens, O God, and above all the earth Thy glory” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore exalted above the Heavens, O God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, God exalted above the Heavens we see not, but we believe: but above all the earth His glory to be not only we believe, but also see.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what kind of madness heretics are afflicted with, I pray you observe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They being cut off from the bond of the Church of Christ, and to a part holding, the whole losing, will not communicate with the whole earth, where is spread abroad the glory of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But we Catholics are in all the earth, because with all the world we communicate, wherever the Glory of Christ is spread abroad.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we see that which then was sung, now fulfilled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There hath been exalted above the Heavens our God, and above all the earth the Glory of the Same.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O heretical insanity!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which thou seest not thou believest with me, that which thou seest thou deniest: thou believest with me in Christ exalted above the Heavens, a thing which we see not; and deniest His Glory over all the earth, a thing which we see.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 12. …Let your Love see the Lord speaking to us, and exhorting us by His example: “A trap they have prepared for My feet, and have bowed down My Soul” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They wished to bring It down as if from Heaven, and to the lower places to weigh It down: “They have bowed My Soul: they have digged before My face a pit and themselves have fallen into it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Me have they hurt, or themselves?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold He hath been exalted above the Heavens, God, and behold above all the earth the Glory of the Same: the kingdom of Christ we see, where is the kingdom of the Jews?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since therefore they did that which to have done they ought not, there hath been done in their case that which to have suffered they ought: themselves have dug a ditch, and themselves have fallen into it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For their persecuting Christ, to Christ did no hurt, but to themselves did hurt.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And do not suppose, brethren, that themselves alone hath this befallen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Every one that prepareth a pit for his brother, it must needs be that himself fall into it.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the patience of good men with preparation of heart accepteth the will of God: and glorieth in tribulations, saying that which followeth: “Prepared is my heart, O God, I will sing and play” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What hath he done to me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath prepared a pit, my heart is prepared.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath prepared pit to deceive, shall I not prepare heart to suffer?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath prepared pit to oppress, shall I not prepare heart to endure?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore he shall fall into it, but I will sing and play.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the heart prepared in an Apostle, because he hath imitated his Lord: “We glory,” he saith, “in tribulations: because tribulation worketh patience: patience probation, probation hope, but hope maketh not ashamed: because the love of God is shed abroad in our hearts through the Holy Spirit, which hath been given to us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was in oppressions, in chains, in prisons, in stripes, in hunger and thirst, in cold and nakedness, in every wasting of toils and pains, and he was saying, “We glory in tribulations.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence, but that prepared was his heart?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore he was singing and playing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Rise up, my glory” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that had fled from the face of Saul into a cavern, saith, “Rise up, my glory:” glorified be Jesus after His Passion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Rise up, psaltery and harp.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He calleth upon what to rise?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Two organs I see: but Body of Christ one I see, one flesh hath risen again, and two organs have risen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The one organ then is the psaltery, the other the harp.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Organs is the word used for all instruments of musicians.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not only is that called an organ, which is great, and blown into with bellows; but whatsoever is adapted to playing and is corporeal, whereof for an instrument the player maketh use, is said to be an organ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But distinguished from one another are these organs.…What therefore do these two organs figure to us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Christ the Lord our God is waking up His psaltery and His harp; and He saith, “I will rise up at the dawn.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I suppose that here ye now perceive the Lord rising.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have read thereof in the Gospel: see the hour of the Resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How long through shadows was Christ being sought?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath shone, be He acknowledged; “at the dawn” He rose again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is psaltery? what is harp?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Through His flesh two kinds of deeds the Lord hath wrought, miracles and sufferings: miracles from above have been, sufferings from below have been.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But those miracles which He did were divine; but through Body He did them, through flesh He did them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The flesh therefore working things divine, is the psaltery: the flesh suffering things human is the harp.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the psaltery sound, let the blind be enlightened, let the deaf hear, let the paralytics be braced to strength, the lame walk, the sick rise up, the dead rise again; this is the sound of the Psaltery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let there sound also the harp, let Him hunger, thirst, sleep, be held, scourged, derided, crucified, buried.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When therefore thou seest in that Flesh certain things to have sounded from above, certain things from the lower part, one flesh hath risen again, and in one flesh we acknowledge both psaltery and harp.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And these two kinds of things done have fulfilled the Gospel, and it is preached in the nations: for both the miracles and the sufferings of the Lord are preached.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore there hath risen psaltery and harp in the dawn, and he confesseth to the Lord; and saith what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will confess to Thee among the peoples, O Lord, and will play to Thee among the nations: for magnified even unto the Heavens hath been Thy mercy, and even unto the clouds Thy truth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Heavens above clouds, and clouds below heavens: and nevertheless to this nearest heaven belong clouds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But sometimes clouds rest upon the mountains, even so far in the nearest air are they rolled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But a Heaven above there is, the habitations of Angels, Thrones, Dominions, Principalities, Powers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This therefore may perchance seem to be what should have been said: “Unto the Heavens Thy truth, and even unto the clouds Thy mercy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in Heaven Angels praise God, seeing the very form of truth, without any darkness of vision, without any admixture of unreality: they see, love, praise, are not wearied.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is truth: but here in our own misery surely there is mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to a miserable one must be rendered mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is no need of mercy above, where is no miserable one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have said this because that it seemeth as though it might have been more fittingly said, “Magnified even unto the Heavens hath been Thy truth, and even unto the clouds Thy mercy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “clouds” we understand to be preachers of truth, men bearing that flesh in a manner dark, whence God both gleameth in miracles, and thundereth in precepts.…Glory to our Lord, and to the Mercy of the Same, and to the Truth of the Same, because neither hath He forsaken by mercy to make us blessed through His Grace, nor defrauded us of truth: because first Truth veiled in flesh came to us and healed through His flesh the interior eye of our heart, in order that hereafter face to face we may be able to see It.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Giving therefore to Him thanks, let us say with the same Psalm the last verses, which sometime since too I have said, “Be Thou exalted above the Heavens, O God, and above all the earth Thy glory” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this to Him the Prophet said so many years before; this now we see; this therefore let us also say.
